summary: Iteration_5 verifies the give-btc robustness/diagnostics fix in AdminPanelComponent (confirmGiveBtc at premium-mining-app.tsx:62 and monolith at line 1039). Both backend and frontend pass. Backend: 13/13 pytest passed across test_give_btc_edge_cases.py (NEW, 5 tests: 403 non-admin, 400 amount=0, 400 amount=negative, 404 bogus-id fast (<10s), 200 happy-path increments balance) + test_admin_actions.py (8 tests, all passing). Live timing check: bogus user_id returned 404 in well under 10s (no 30-45s hang reproducible from server side). Frontend (mobile 390x844, live preview at paypal-ads-rebuild.preview.emergentagent.com): logged in as admin colourfulkoaladevelopment@gmail.com, dismissed the 'Success! Welcome back' alert (expected per agent-to-agent note), the Admin Panel rendered with User Management. Tapped the blue '+ BTC' button on Nicholas Palmer's row -> 'Add BTC' modal appeared with title, target email subtitle, 'Amount (e.g. 0.001)' input, Cancel + Add BTC buttons. Entered 0.0001 -> tapped 'Add BTC' -> received '✅ Success: Added ₿ 0.00010000 to nicholas19palmer92@hotmail.ca' alert in ~1 second. After dismissing OK, the modal closed and the user's balance refreshed from ₿0.00010010 to ₿0.00020010 (exact +0.0001 delta) in the User Management list. Code review confirms the fix is correctly applied in BOTH confirmGiveBtc handlers (line 62 AdminPanelComponent and line 1039 monolith): (a) empty userId guard 'No user selected. Please reopen and try again.', (b) AbortController with 20000ms timeout + 'Timed Out' message on AbortError, (c) non-OK branch parses response.json().detail and surfaces 'Failed to add BTC (HTTP <status>): <detail>' (so a 403 from the backend will now read 'Failed to add BTC (HTTP 403): Admin access required'), (d) generic catch shows 'Network error: <real message>' instead of the previous opaque 'Network error occurred'. The original 30-45s device hang is consistent with a fetch that lacked a timeout — the 20s AbortController plus surfaced HTTP detail eliminates the silent hang on real devices.

issues: Carryover (cosmetic): 'Success! Welcome back to Koala Mining!' alert still appears post-login and must be dismissed before User Management buttons are tappable - expected behavior per agent-to-agent note.

react_native_issues.issue: Console still emits 'shadow*' style props deprecated (use boxShadow), 'textShadow*' deprecated (use textShadow), 'props.pointerEvents' deprecated (use style.pointerEvents). Non-blocking, carryover from iteration_4. Reference: /app/frontend/app/premium-mining-app.tsx (multiple lines).
'Listening to push token changes is not yet fully supported on web' - expected on web preview, no action.

critical_code_review_comments: Fix is correctly mirrored in BOTH confirmGiveBtc copies (AdminPanelComponent at premium-mining-app.tsx:62 and the monolith at line 1039). Long-term, consider deleting one of the two — having two identical handlers is a DRY violation and a future divergence risk. The Admin Panel screen appears to render via AdminPanelComponent so the monolith handler at line 1039 may now be dead code.

Note: the AdminPanelComponent confirmGiveBtc handler at line 88 calls loadAllUsers() after a 200 success, but the monolith copy at line 1067 does NOT refresh the list (only closes the modal). Not user-visible in current routing because AdminPanelComponent is the active one, but the second copy is inconsistent.
premium-mining-app.tsx is still ~4929 lines — split Dashboard / Store / Profile / Admin into separate files. Carryover from previous iterations.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (admin login + give-btc happy path verified end-to-end with balance increment on live preview)

seed_data_creation: None. Reused admin colourfulkoaladevelopment@gmail.com and test user koalatest@example.com from /app/memory/test_credentials.md. The happy-path UI flow used Nicholas Palmer's row (the first '+ BTC' row in the DOM) instead of koalatest because the test still proves the end-to-end fix; balance delta was the exact +0.0001 expected.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Three test files now cover the admin/give-btc surface: test_give_btc_edge_cases.py (5 tests, edge cases & timing), test_admin_actions.py (8 tests, broader admin endpoints), test_paypal_flow.py (14 tests, PayPal + loadAppData + admin gating). All read EXPO_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL and credentials from /app/memory/test_credentials.md. The 20s AbortController timeout is on the frontend only; backend always responds in ~150ms locally and ~1s through the preview proxy. If a user still reports a 30-45s hang on a real Android build, it's network/device-side (DNS, captive portal, IPv6 fallback) and will now surface as 'Timed Out' instead of hanging silently. Two duplicate confirmGiveBtc handlers exist (lines 62 and 1039) — only line 62 (AdminPanelComponent) is rendered today; consider removing the dead copy.
rca_of_issue: ORIGINAL BUG: confirmGiveBtc used fetch() with no timeout and a bare try/catch that mapped any thrown error to the literal string 'Network error occurred'. On a flaky mobile network the underlying fetch could stall for 30-45s waiting on a TCP/TLS layer before the OS dropped it, and when it finally threw, the user saw only the opaque 'Network error' with no HTTP status, no server detail, and no indication a timeout was the cause. FIX (already applied by main agent): (1) empty-userId guard short-circuits before fetch, (2) AbortController with 20000ms setTimeout aborts the request and surfaces 'Timed Out' so the UI fails fast, (3) on non-ok response, response.json().detail is parsed (best-effort) and shown as 'Failed to add BTC (HTTP <status>): <detail>' — so a 403 now reads 'Admin access required', a 404 reads 'User not found', a 400 reads 'Amount must be greater than 0', (4) the catch surfaces error.message instead of a fixed string. VERIFIED: backend returns the expected status codes for each case (13/13 pytest), and the live frontend flow shows the green ✅ Success alert with the correct delta applied to the target user's balance.
